---
title: "Building a Local-First Distributed Cache in Rust"
subtitle: "Designing HydraCache from local cache core to distributed coordination"
author: "Artur Buzov"
---

# Preface {.unnumbered}

This book follows the design and implementation of HydraCache: a Rust-native cache library that starts as a convenient local cache and grows toward database result caching and distributed synchronization.

The goal is not only to document HydraCache. The goal is to explain the systems ideas behind it:

- local cache ergonomics
- key design
- TTL and eviction
- invalidation
- query result caching
- duplicate load suppression
- distributed cache coordination

The source material lives next to the code:

- [Knowledge process](../KNOWLEDGE_PROCESS.md)
- [Learning track](../learning/00_learning_track.md)
- [Unified architecture](../../HYDRACACHE_UNIFIED_ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [Reference reread index](../../HYDRACACHE_REFERENCE_REREAD_INDEX.md)

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-book-pipeline
%%| fig-cap: "How project knowledge becomes book material."
flowchart LR
  A[Development Work] --> B[Development Log]
  B --> C[Learning Notes]
  C --> D[ADR and Synthesis]
  D --> E[Book Chapter]
```
